title: Mellow Spicy Mexican Hind Shank Chiles Rellenos
description: A spicy, pillowy, lazy-sunday Mexican dish featuring hind shank, served in a ceramic tagine, inspired by Jalisco-style cuisine and Ayurvedic tamasic-friendly principles.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the hind shank with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat and sear the hind shank until browned on all sides.
Add the onion and garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the tomato paste, chipotle peppers, and roasted poblano peppers to the pot, stirring to combine.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven to braise the hind shank for 2 1/2 hours.
Remove the pot from the oven and let the hind shank cool in the braising liquid.
Once the hind shank is cool enough to handle, shred it into bite-sized pieces and stir in the queso fresco and chopped cilantro.
Stuff each roasted poblano pepper with the hind shank mixture and place it in a ceramic tagine.
Serve the chiles rellenos hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
